session_1.speaker: Alex
session_1.text: So I just started at TechCorp about three weeks ago. I'm a Junior Developer on the payments team.

session_1.speaker: Sam
session_1.text: Congrats on the new role! How's it going so far?

session_1.speaker: Alex
session_1.text: The onboarding was a bit rough — I got a laptop with the wrong OS image, so I spent the first two days just setting up my environment.

session_1.speaker: Sam
session_1.text: That's annoying. What did your manager say?

session_1.speaker: Alex
session_1.text: My manager is Bob. He's been at TechCorp for six years, started as an intern and worked his way up to Engineering Manager. He manages twelve people across two squads. He was very chill about the whole setup situation.

session_1.speaker: Sam
session_1.text: Good to have a patient manager. Where is the office?

session_1.speaker: Alex
session_1.text: Downtown Portland, right next to a food cart pod on Alder Street. Honestly that's the best perk so far. I eat there almost every day.

session_1.speaker: Alex
session_1.text: Mostly Go and React. There's some internal tooling with basically zero documentation. Bob is going to pair with me next week to walk through the custom deployment pipeline.

session_1.speaker: Sam
session_1.text: Sounds like a good environment to grow in. Anything else?

session_1.speaker: Alex
session_1.text: They use Slack for literally everything — even stuff that should probably be in Jira. But I'm getting used to it.

session_1.speaker: Sam
session_1.text: Classic startup culture. Let me know how the pairing session goes.

session_2.speaker: Alex
session_2.text: Big news — I got promoted to Senior Developer! It happened way faster than I expected.

session_2.speaker: Alex
session_2.text: There was a production incident last month. Our payment processing pipeline went down during peak hours. I figured out the root cause — a race condition in the queue consumer that only shows up under high load.

session_2.speaker: Sam
session_2.text: Nice catch. How did you fix it?

session_2.speaker: Alex
session_2.text: I wrote a detailed post-mortem and proposed switching to a distributed lock pattern. The VP of Engineering read it and was apparently impressed. Bob put me up for the promotion right after.

session_2.speaker: Alex
session_2.text: Pay bump — not life-changing, but enough that rent isn't stressful anymore. And I'm now tech lead for the new checkout redesign project.

session_2.speaker: Alex
session_2.text: Kind of. I'll be working with the design team directly and I've never done that before. Bob said he'll mentor me through it.

session_2.speaker: Sam
session_2.text: You'll be fine. Anything else change?

session_2.speaker: Alex
session_2.text: I moved desks. Now I'm sitting next to the window with a view of the West Hills.

session_3.speaker: Alex
session_3.text: Wild week. TechCorp got acquired by MegaCorp. The announcement came Monday morning in an all-hands that nobody saw coming — even Bob seemed surprised.

session_3.speaker: Alex
session_3.text: Massive enterprise software conglomerate based out of Seattle. Twenty thousand employees, fifty countries. The CEO did a video call and said all the usual stuff about preserving culture and synergies.

session_3.speaker: Alex
session_3.text: Not really. Immediate changes are mostly administrative — switching to MegaCorp's benefits provider, which actually has better dental coverage, so that's a win. Email domains are migrating too, so I'll be alex@megacorp.com instead of alex@techcorp.com.

session_3.speaker: Alex
session_3.text: Bob is moving to HR. MegaCorp has a People Operations division and they wanted someone with deep knowledge of TechCorp's engineering org. He seemed conflicted but said it's a good career opportunity.

session_3.speaker: Alex
session_3.text: Someone named Diane, transferring from MegaCorp's Seattle office. I haven't met her yet.

session_3.speaker: Alex
session_3.text: On hold until the integration settles. Frustrating, but understandable.

session_3.speaker: Sam
session_3.text: Hang in there. Big changes take time.

session_4.speaker: Alex
session_4.text: Quick update — Diane has been my manager for about two months now and honestly she's great. Very different from Bob, but in a good way. She's much more structured, does weekly one-on-ones with a proper agenda.

session_4.speaker: Sam
session_4.text: Sounds like a step up in terms of organisation. Any news on the project?

session_4.speaker: Alex
session_4.text: She pushed hard to get the checkout redesign restarted. I'm grateful because it was just sitting in limbo.

session_4.speaker: Sam
session_4.text: What about the office situation with the acquisition?

session_4.speaker: Alex
session_4.text: MegaCorp decided to consolidate all Portland engineering teams into their existing Seattle campus. They gave everyone the option to relocate or go fully remote.

session_4.speaker: Alex
session_4.text: Remote. My girlfriend just signed a two-year lease on our Portland apartment and we don't want to move. About half the team relocated though, so there's been a lot of turnover.

session_4.speaker: Sam
session_4.text: How's the technical side of things going?

session_4.speaker: Alex
session_4.text: We're migrating everything to MegaCorp's cloud infrastructure. They use Azure exclusively — new for me since TechCorp was all-in on AWS. Steep learning curve, but Diane has been patient about it.

session_4.speaker: Sam
session_4.text: Anything else going on in life outside work?

session_4.speaker: Alex
session_4.text: I adopted a dog last weekend! His name is Pixel, he's a two-year-old corgi mix and he is the cutest thing alive.

session_4.speaker: Sam
session_4.text: That's the best update yet. What cloud did TechCorp use before?

session_4.speaker: Alex
session_4.text: AWS. The move to Azure is a big shift but I'm getting there.

qa.question: What was Alex's original job title at TechCorp?
What is Alex's current job title after the promotion?
What caused the production incident that led to Alex's promotion?
qa.answer: A race condition in the queue consumer that only manifested under high load.

qa.question: Who was Alex's manager before the MegaCorp acquisition, and what role did that person move to?
qa.answer: Bob was the manager; he moved to HR / People Operations after the MegaCorp acquisition.

qa.question: Who is Alex's current manager?
qa.answer: Diane, who transferred from MegaCorp's Seattle office.

qa.question: What project was Alex leading that got put on hold and then restarted?
What cloud platform did TechCorp use, and what platform is MegaCorp migrating to?
qa.answer: TechCorp used AWS; MegaCorp uses Azure exclusively.

qa.question: What is Alex's current work arrangement after the acquisition?
Does Alex have any pets, and if so what are the details?
qa.answer: Yes, a dog named Pixel, a two-year-old corgi mix.

qa.question: In what order did Alex's job titles change over time?
qa.answer: Junior Developer, then Senior Developer (promoted after the payment pipeline incident).
